title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our authentic Indian recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a homemade feast!

Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Butter Chicken

seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with rice and naan, garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian dish perfect for any occasion.

description: Savor the rich and creamy flavors of Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian classic known for its tender chicken simmered in a luscious tomato-based sauce. This Murgh Makhani recipe delivers an authentic taste experience with aromatic spices and a velvety finish, perfect for a comforting meal. It's a culinary journey that promises warmth and satisfaction in every bite.

ingredients.notes: or Kashmiri chili powder for color
soaked in warm water for 15 minutes
or to taste, to balance acidity

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, turmeric powder, red chili powder, 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Mix well to coat the chicken evenly. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces and cook in batches until lightly browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per batch. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Sauce
instructions.text: Add the crushed tomatoes to the pan. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens and the raw tomato smell disappears. Stir in the remaining 1/2 teaspoon garam masala and the sugar.

instructions.title: Blend for Creamy Texture
instructions.text: Drain the soaked cashews and add them to the tomato mixture. Carefully transfer the sauce to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to do this in batches. For an extra smooth sauce, pass it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the smooth sauce to the pan over low heat. Add the cooked chicken pieces and stir to combine. Pour in the heavy cream and gently mix. Crush the dried fenugreek leaves between your palms and add them to the curry. Simmer for another 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot with basmati rice, naan bread, or roti. Enjoy this classic Indian delicacy!

tips: For extra tender chicken, marinate it overnight. The yogurt helps to tenderize the meat beautifully.
If you prefer a spicier Butter Chicken, add a slit green chili along with the onions, or increase the amount of red chili powder in the marinade.
Always blend the sauce until perfectly smooth for that restaurant-style velvety texture. Straining it through a sieve after blending ensures no lumps remain.
article:
Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani: Your Guide to India's Beloved Dish

Few dishes evoke the warmth, richness, and pure culinary delight quite like Butter Chicken. Known globally as Murgh Makhani, this iconic Indian curry has captivated palates with its velvety tomato and cream sauce, tender chicken, and perfectly balanced sweet and tangy notes. Originating from the bustling streets of Delhi, it has transcended its humble beginnings to become a staple on menus worldwide, celebrated for its comforting flavors and luxurious texture. While often enjoyed in restaurants, crafting authentic Butter Chicken at home is an incredibly rewarding experience, allowing you to control the freshness and quality of every ingredient.

This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know about preparing a classic Murgh Makhani. From understanding its rich history to mastering the marinade and perfecting the luscious sauce, you’ll gain the confidence to recreate this culinary masterpiece in your own kitchen. Get ready to embark on a flavorful journey that promises to tantalize your taste buds and impress your family and friends with one of India's most cherished chicken recipes.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally meaning "chicken with butter," is a quintessential Indian curry where marinated chicken is simmered in a rich, creamy, and mildly spiced tomato-based sauce. Its origins trace back to the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant in Daryaganj, Delhi, where Kundan Lal Gujral famously experimented with leftover tandoori chicken, tossing it in a tomato and butter gravy. This innovative creation quickly became a sensation, laying the foundation for one of the world's most beloved Indian dishes.

The hallmark of a great Murgh Makhani lies in its exquisite balance of flavors. The sauce, often referred to as 'makhani gravy,' is characterized by its smooth texture and harmonious blend of tanginess from tomatoes, sweetness from a touch of sugar, and richness from generous amounts of butter and cream. Aromatic spices like ginger, garlic, garam masala, and crucially, kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), contribute to its distinct and irresistible aroma. The chicken, traditionally cooked in a tandoor oven, is succulent and often bears a smoky char that adds another layer of complexity to the dish. Together, these elements create a symphony of taste that makes Butter Chicken an unparalleled culinary experience.

## Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a selection of fresh, high-quality ingredients for both the chicken marinade and the signature makhani sauce. Don't be intimidated by the list; most items are readily available and contribute to the authentic flavor profile.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color, regular for heat)
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1 tablespoon lemon juice

For the Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter, plus more for finishing
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 large fresh tomatoes, pureed
*   1/2 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes, optional but recommended for smoothness)
*   1 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1 teaspoon coriander powder
*   1/2 teaspoon red chili powder (adjust to taste)
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1 teaspoon sugar (or to taste, balances tanginess)
*   1/2 teaspoon salt (or to taste)
*   1/2 cup heavy cream, plus more for garnish
*   1 tablespoon kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed between palms
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions to Make Butter Chicken

Follow these detailed steps to create a show-stopping Butter Chicken that will impress even the most discerning palates.

Step 1: Marinate the Chicken
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to ensure every piece is coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor.

Step 2: Cook the Marinated Chicken
Heat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet or non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side, until browned and cooked through. The chicken doesn't need to be fully charred, just nicely cooked. Remove the chicken from the pan and set aside.

Step 3: Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base
In the same pan, melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until translucent and softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or fresh tomato puree), soaked cashews, cumin powder, coriander powder, and 1/2 teaspoon red chili powder. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have broken down and the sauce has thickened slightly.

Step 4: Blend the Sauce
Remove the pan from the heat and let the sauce cool slightly. Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender or food processor. Blend until incredibly smooth and creamy. For an extra velvety texture, you can pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This removes any small bits of onion or cashew, ensuring a restaurant-quality finish for your Butter Chicken.

Step 5: Finish the Sauce
Return the blended and strained sauce to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the remaining 1/2 teaspoon garam masala, sugar, and salt. Stir well. Simmer for 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.
Stir in the heavy cream and a generous knob of butter. Continue to simmer gently for another 2-3 minutes, ensuring the sauce doesn't boil rapidly, as this can cause the cream to separate. Crush the kasuri methi between your palms and add it to the sauce. This ingredient is crucial for the authentic flavor of Murgh Makhani.

Step 6: Combine and Serve
Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering makhani sauce. Stir gently to coat the chicken completely. Simmer for another 5 minutes to allow the chicken to absorb the flavors of the rich sauce.
Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with a swirl of heavy cream and fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken Results

Achieving truly exceptional Butter Chicken involves a few key techniques that can elevate your dish from good to extraordinary.

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't rush the marination process. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will become. Overnight marination is ideal.
*   **Achieve Smokiness:** Traditionally, Murgh Makhani uses tandoor-cooked chicken. If you don't have a tandoor, pan-frying until slightly charred or grilling the chicken can impart a similar smoky depth. For an authentic smoky flavor, consider the 'dhungar' method: place a small heat-proof bowl with a burning charcoal in the chicken pot, drizzle with ghee, and cover for a few minutes.
*   **Blend and Strain:** For the silkiest, restaurant-quality sauce, always blend the makhani base until super smooth. Straining it through a fine-mesh sieve after blending will remove any fibrous bits, resulting in an incredibly luxurious texture.
*   **Don't Skimp on Butter and Cream:** While you can modify for health, the classic Butter Chicken relies on generous amounts of butter and cream for its characteristic richness and mouthfeel.
*   **Kasuri Methi is Essential:** Dried fenugreek leaves (kasuri methi) provide an unmistakable aroma and subtle bitterness that is fundamental to the authentic flavor of Murgh Makhani. Don't skip it!
*   **Balance Flavors:** Taste your sauce frequently. The perfect Butter Chicken should have a harmonious balance of sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy notes. Adjust sugar and salt as needed.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice preparations. Here are some classic serving suggestions to complete your meal:

*   **Naan Bread:** Soft, fluffy naan is perhaps the most popular accompaniment, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich sauce. Garlic naan or butter naan are particularly delicious choices.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or a fragrant jeera (cumin) rice provides a neutral base that complements the intensity of the curry.
*   **Roti or Paratha:** For a lighter option, whole wheat roti or flaky paratha are excellent choices.
*   **Fresh Garnish:** Always finish your Murgh Makhani with a swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of chopped fresh cilantro for both visual appeal and a burst of freshness.
*   **Sides:** A simple side salad, a cooling raita (yogurt dip), or some pickled onions can offer a refreshing contrast to the richness of the Butter Chicken.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken

While the classic Butter Chicken is a beloved masterpiece, there are several ways to adapt the recipe to suit different dietary needs or preferences without sacrificing flavor.

*   **Vegetarian Alternatives:** Easily transform this dish into Paneer Makhani by substituting chicken with cubes of paneer (Indian cottage cheese), or Tofu Makhani for a vegan-friendly version (ensure to use plant-based cream and butter substitutes, and marinate tofu similarly). Mixed vegetable makhani, using cauliflower, peas, and potatoes, is another delicious option.
*   **Healthier Options:** To reduce calories, you can use Greek yogurt in the marinade and a lighter cream (like half-and-half) or even coconut milk in the sauce. Baking or air-frying the chicken instead of pan-frying also cuts down on oil.
*   **Adjusting Spiciness:** For more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder in both the marinade and the sauce. For a milder version, reduce it or omit it entirely, relying on Kashmiri chili powder for color without much heat.
*   **Nut-Free Version:** If you have a nut allergy, you can substitute the cashews with a tablespoon or two of poppy seeds (soaked and blended) or melon seeds for a similar thickening effect, or simply use a bit more tomato paste and cream to achieve the desired consistency.

Making authentic Butter Chicken at home is a culinary journey that rewards you with incredible flavors and aromas. By following these steps and tips, you can recreate the magic of Murgh Makhani, bringing a taste of India's beloved classic to your dining table. Enjoy the process, savor the rich flavors, and delight in the satisfaction of mastering this iconic dish.
